WorkspaceConfig API plumbed through build_project_call_graph but every one of the 18 production callers passes None; feature is dead code

discoveries.finding: ParserPool in crates/tldr-core/src/ast/parser.rs:40-42 uses LANGUAGE_TYPESCRIPT for both TypeScript and JavaScript, which does not parse JSX. The callgraph subsystem correctly uses LANGUAGE_TSX (cross-file_types.rs:644, languages/mod.rs:543) — but quality/smells/patterns all go through ParserPool and produce pathological error-recovery trees on .tsx files, causing the message-chain detector's recursive traversal to exponentially blow up

detect_ts_base_url and detect_ts_paths in crates/tldr-core/src/callgraph/module_index.rs:1025-1059 only read <root>/tsconfig.json with no scanning for sibling tsconfigs. A pnpm monorepo with per-package tsconfigs cannot be handled by running at the workspace root

The impact_analysis_with_ast_fallback message 'Function found via AST but has no call edges in analyzed scope' (crates/tldr-core/src/analysis/impact.rs:158) is emitted whenever the call-graph lookup fails, including the common case where the function has callers but the call graph failed to resolve them — making the note actively misleading on pnpm monorepos

issues: change-impact default to GitHead with silent empty-success shape collapses three distinct failure modes (clean tree, git unavailable, explicit session mode) into identical output
message-chain detector is exponential on tree-sitter error-recovery trees (root cause: wrong grammar)
impact/whatbreaks lie softly when @/ alias resolution misses
